edges.description: Defective MMUT/AdoCbl function blocks conversion of methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA.
Blocked methylmalonyl-CoA mutase activity causes methylmalonic acid accumulation.
Propionate-pathway flux produces elevated plasma propionylcarnitine during MMA diagnosis and monitoring.
intermediate_mechanisms: Propionyl-CoA accumulation is buffered by carnitine conjugation.

edges.description: Propionyl-CoA overflow generates methylcitric acid, a disease-specific organic acid marker.
intermediate_mechanisms: Propionyl-CoA condenses with oxaloacetate to form methylcitrate.

edges.description: Propionyl-CoA is diverted through beta-oxidation to urinary 3-hydroxypropionate.
Accumulating organic acids and anaplerotic failure create episodic metabolic crises under catabolic stress.
Accumulated metabolites and reduced succinyl-CoA input impair the TCA cycle and oxidative phosphorylation.
intermediate_mechanisms: Accumulating primary and secondary organic acid metabolites

edges.description: Enlarged methylmalonyl-CoA and related acyl-CoA pools drive mitochondrial protein hyperacylation.
MUT deficiency perturbs lysosomal and autophagy regulation in cellular MMA models.
Pancreatitis is a reported complication of MMA and other branched-chain organic acidemias; the intervening mechanism is not resolved in the cited evidence.
Low bone density is a chronic complication in MMA/PA, but the intervening mechanism is incompletely defined.
Osteoporosis is a chronic skeletal complication in MMA/PA, with unresolved causal intermediates.
Organic acid and ketoacid accumulation during decompensation produces high-anion-gap metabolic acidosis.
Propionyl-CoA and methylmalonyl-CoA interfere with urea-cycle activation, causing secondary hyperammonemia.
Urea-cycle inhibition raises circulating ammonia during decompensation.
Acute decompensation commonly presents with nausea and vomiting.
Decompensation produces altered mental status and lethargy.
Mitochondrial energy failure and metabolite toxicity injure the high-energy CNS compartment.
intermediate_mechanisms: Deficient succinyl-CoA input to the TCA cycle

edges.description: Chronic metabolic energy stress and dietary fragility contribute to poor growth.
Cardiac energy vulnerability and organic-acidemia complications contribute to dilated cardiomyopathy in a subset of MMA patients.
Organic-acidemia cardiac involvement can include acquired long QT syndrome.
High-energy ocular tissue vulnerability contributes to optic neuropathy and vision loss in classical MMA.
Circulating GDF15 is used as a mitochondrial dysfunction biomarker in isolated MMA.
Neurometabolic injury slows developmental trajectory in children with isolated MMA.
Brain tissue damage and neuropsychological deficits manifest as cognitive impairment.
Chronic brain injury and acute metabolic crises can manifest as epilepsy or seizures.
MMA-associated metabolic brain injury targets basal ganglia regions and can present as metabolic stroke.
Metabolic brain injury and metabolic stroke sequelae can manifest as movement disorders.
Metabolic stroke sequelae can include spastic quadri- or paraparesis.
Abnormal adipose methylmalonylation and acyl-CoA accretion are associated with the MMA lipodystrophy phenotype.
intermediate_mechanisms: Altered adipose inflammatory and thermogenic signaling

edges.description: FGF21 tracks mitochondrial/adipose stress associated with acyl-CoA accretion and methylmalonylation.
Lysosomal-autophagy failure impairs clearance of damaged mitochondria and contributes to renal mitochondrial stress.
edges.target: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

edges.description: Renal epithelial mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy contribute to progressive CKD in MMA.
edges.source: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

edges.description: Renal injury from MMA is tracked by LCN2 as a circulating kidney injury biomarker.
edges.source: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

edges.description: Increased serum or urinary methylmalonic acid reports the blocked methylmalonyl-CoA mutase/cofactor pathway.
Elevated propionylcarnitine reports propionate-pathway metabolite accumulation used for MMA diagnosis and newborn screening.
Increased methylcitric acid reports propionyl-CoA overflow into disease-specific organic-acid metabolites.
Increased methylcitrate under renal epithelial metabolic stress reports a potentially toxic, disease-specific metabolite branch relevant to kidney injury models.
edges.source: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

edges.description: Urinary 3-hydroxypropionate reports propionyl-CoA diversion through beta-oxidation during MMA.
Increased FGF21 reports mitochondrial dysfunction and multisystem disease burden in isolated MMA.
FGF21 levels correlate with acyl-CoA accretion and aberrant adipose methylmalonylation in MMA.
Increased GDF15 reports mitochondrial dysfunction and multisystem disease burden in isolated MMA.
Higher biomarker concentrations are associated with severe mut0 and cblB MMA subtypes and reduced propionate oxidation capacity.
Increased LCN2 reports kidney injury downstream of renal mitochondrial stress and toxic organic-acid exposure.
edges.source: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

edges.description: Increased ammonia reports secondary urea-cycle inhibition during acute organic-acid decompensation.
Increased circulating ammonia is the biochemical readout of the hyperammonemia phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: The impact of metabolic stressors on mitochondrial homeostasis in a renal epithelial cell model of methylmalonic aciduria.
evidence.snippet: Methylcitrate as potentially toxic, disease-specific metabolite was increased by HP and I/V load.

readouts.interpretation: Increased methylcitric acid reports propionyl-CoA overflow into disease-specific organic-acid metabolites.
Increased methylcitrate under renal epithelial metabolic stress reports a potentially toxic, disease-specific metabolite branch relevant to kidney injury models.
readouts.target: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

evidence.reference_title: 3-hydroxypropionate: significance of -oxidation of propionate in patients with propionic acidemia and methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: [l-(14)C]Propionate administered intravenously was metabolized to methylmalonate, to 3-hydroxypropionate, and to methylcitrate in the urine of a patient with methylmalonic acidemia.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: In MMA, elevations of plasma propionylcarnitine, glycine, and alanine coupled with elevation of urinary methylmalonic, 3-OH-propionic, and methylcitric acids provide the diagnosis

readouts.interpretation: Urinary 3-hydroxypropionate reports propionyl-CoA diversion through beta-oxidation during MMA.

nodes.description: Excess methylmalonyl-CoA, propionyl-CoA, and malonyl-CoA drive widespread lysine hyperacylation of mitochondrial proteins across TCA cycle, beta-oxidation, amino acid metabolism, urea cycle, and oxidative stress pathways. Reduced levels of mitochondrial sirtuins SIRT3/4/5, particularly SIRT5 which can remove methylmalonylation, amplify dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into the pathophysiology of methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: new observations have revealed that aberrant acylation, specifically methylmalonylation, is a characteristic feature of MMA. The mitochondrial sirtuin enzyme SIRT5 is capable of recognizing and removing this PTM, however, reduced protein levels of SIRT5 along with other mitochondrial SIRTs 3 and 4 in MMA

nodes.description: Catabolic stress in MMA precipitates acute metabolic decompensation with organic acid and ketoacid accumulation, metabolic acidosis, secondary hyperammonemia, vomiting, feeding intolerance, and altered mental status.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: With intercurrent illness, PA and MMA patients typically present with nausea and vomiting, worsening anorexia, and encephalopathy, with laboratory studies demonstrating metabolic acidosis, ketonuria, hyperammonemia, pancytopenia, and electrolyte disturbances

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Accumulation of propionyl-CoA, and to some extent methylmalonyl-CoA, results in secondary inhibition of N-acetylglutamate synthase (NAGS), causing secondary hyperammonemia in these patients

evidence.reference_title: Proposed guidelines for the diagnosis and management of methylmalonic and propionic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Patients present either shortly after birth with acute deterioration, metabolic acidosis and hyperammonemia

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Accumulation of propionyl-CoA, and to some extent methylmalonyl-CoA, results in secondary inhibition of N-acetylglutamate synthase (NAGS), causing secondary hyperammonemia in these patients

readouts.interpretation: Increased ammonia reports secondary urea-cycle inhibition during acute organic-acid decompensation.
Increased circulating ammonia is the biochemical readout of the hyperammonemia phenotype.

nodes.description: Basal ganglia injury (metabolic stroke-like injury), particularly involving basal ganglia regions, is a recognized neurological complication during acute metabolic crises.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Patients with organic acidopathies suffer metabolic brain injury that targets specific regions of the basal ganglia in a distinctive pattern

nodes.description: Progressive chronic kidney disease is a major long-term complication of MMA, mechanistically linked to proximal tubular mitochondrial dysfunction, oxidative stress, and impaired mitophagy.

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by life-threatening episodes of ketoacidosis, chronic kidney disease, and other multiorgan complications.

evidence.reference_title: The impact of metabolic stressors on mitochondrial homeostasis in a renal epithelial cell model of methylmalonic aciduria.
evidence.snippet: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a well-known long-term complication.

nodes.description: Dilated cardiomyopathy occurs in a subset of MMA patients. In a 2000-2022 pediatric cohort, cardiac involvement occurred in approximately 38% of PA/MMA patients, with DCM predominant.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiac Involvement in Classical Organic Acidurias: Clinical Profile and Outcome in a Pediatric Cohort.
evidence.snippet: Cardiac anomalies were found in 23/60 OA patients, all with PA or MMA, represented by DCM (17/23 patients)

evidence.reference_title: Cardiac Involvement in Classical Organic Acidurias: Clinical Profile and Outcome in a Pediatric Cohort.
evidence.snippet: 35% in MMA with cardiomyopathy

evidence.reference_title: Lipodystrophy in methylmalonic acidemia associated with elevated FGF21 and abnormal methylmalonylation.
evidence.snippet: Fibroblast growth factor 21 levels were associated with acyl-CoA accretion, aberrant methylmalonylation in adipose tissue, and an attenuated inflammatory cytokine profile.

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Biomarker concentrations are higher in patients with the severe mut0 -type and cblB-type MMA

readouts.interpretation: Increased FGF21 reports mitochondrial dysfunction and multisystem disease burden in isolated MMA.
FGF21 levels correlate with acyl-CoA accretion and aberrant adipose methylmalonylation in MMA.

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: We have therefore explored the use of the 1-13 C-propionate oxidation breath test (POBT) to measure metabolic capacity and the changes in circulating proteins to assess mitochondrial dysfunction (fibroblast growth factor 21 FGF21 and growth differentiation factor 15 GDF15) and kidney injury (lipocalin-2 LCN2).

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Biomarker concentrations are higher in patients with the severe mut0 -type and cblB-type MMA

readouts.interpretation: Increased GDF15 reports mitochondrial dysfunction and multisystem disease burden in isolated MMA.
Higher biomarker concentrations are associated with severe mut0 and cblB MMA subtypes and reduced propionate oxidation capacity.

nodes.description: Delayed developmental trajectory, particularly in severe mut0 forms. Mean IQ for mut0 enzymatic subtype is rarely above 85.

evidence.reference_title: Review of neuropsychological outcomes in isolated methylmalonic acidemia: recommendations for assessing impact of treatments.
evidence.snippet: Children with this disorder still are at risk for developmental delay, cognitive difficulties and progressive declines in functioning.

evidence.reference_title: Review of neuropsychological outcomes in isolated methylmalonic acidemia: recommendations for assessing impact of treatments.
evidence.snippet: Mean IQ for all types apart from cblA defect enzymatic subtype is rarely above 85

nodes.description: Secondary hyperammonemia during acute metabolic decompensation, related to inhibition of N-acetylglutamate synthase by propionyl-CoA.

evidence.reference_title: Proposed guidelines for the diagnosis and management of methylmalonic and propionic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Patients present either shortly after birth with acute deterioration, metabolic acidosis and hyperammonemia

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Accumulation of propionyl-CoA, and to some extent methylmalonyl-CoA, results in secondary inhibition of N-acetylglutamate synthase (NAGS), causing secondary hyperammonemia in these patients

nodes.description: Defects in methylmalonyl-CoA mutase or cobalamin-dependent cofactor pathways block conversion of methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA, disrupting mitochondrial anaplerosis and the TCA cycle.

evidence.reference_title: Proposed guidelines for the diagnosis and management of methylmalonic and propionic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemia (MMA/PA) are inborn errors of metabolism characterized by accumulation of propionic acid and/or methylmalonic acid due to deficiency of methylmalonyl-CoA mutase (MUT) or propionyl-CoA carboxylase (PCC).

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic acid in aging and disease.
evidence.snippet: MMA is normally present at low levels in the body, but increased levels can come from different sources, such as vitamin B12 deficiency, genetic mutations in enzymes related to the propionate pathway

nodes.description: Cognitive impairment is common in severe MMA subtypes, with processing speed, working memory, language, and attention particularly affected.

evidence.reference_title: Review of neuropsychological outcomes in isolated methylmalonic acidemia: recommendations for assessing impact of treatments.
evidence.snippet: Children with this disorder still are at risk for developmental delay, cognitive difficulties and progressive declines in functioning.

nodes.description: Reduced alertness during metabolic decompensation episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: These decompensation events typically present with decreased oral intake or enteral feeding intolerance, vomiting, and altered mental status or lethargy.

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: We have therefore explored the use of the 1-13 C-propionate oxidation breath test (POBT) to measure metabolic capacity and the changes in circulating proteins to assess mitochondrial dysfunction (fibroblast growth factor 21 FGF21 and growth differentiation factor 15 GDF15) and kidney injury (lipocalin-2 LCN2).

readouts.interpretation: Increased LCN2 reports kidney injury downstream of renal mitochondrial stress and toxic organic-acid exposure.
readouts.target: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

nodes.description: A distinct adipose tissue distribution pattern resembling familial multiple lipomatosis, with centripetal obesity, proximal extremity fat deposition, and paucity of visceral fat. Associated with elevated FGF21 and aberrant methylmalonylation in adipose tissue.

evidence.reference_title: Lipodystrophy in methylmalonic acidemia associated with elevated FGF21 and abnormal methylmalonylation.
evidence.snippet: A distinct adipose tissue distribution pattern was observed in patients with methylmalonyl-CoA mutase deficiency, an inborn error of branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) metabolism, characterized by centripetal obesity with proximal upper and lower extremity fat deposition and paucity of visceral fat

nodes.description: MUT deficiency directly perturbs the lysosome-autophagy system, causing enlarged lysosomes with reduced acidity and degradative capacity, impaired autophagosome-lysosome fusion, and severely reduced autophagic flux. These defects are pharmacologically reversible with anti-propionigenic compounds.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic acidemia triggers lysosomal-autophagy dysfunctions.
evidence.snippet: Lysosomes of MMA cells present as enlarged vacuoles with low degradative capabilities. Notwithstanding, treatment with an anti-propionigenic drug is capable of totally rescuing lysosomal morphology and functional activity in MUT-deficient cells.

evidence.reference_title: Review of neuropsychological outcomes in isolated methylmalonic acidemia: recommendations for assessing impact of treatments.
evidence.snippet: These other disorders include enzymatic subtypes related to cobalamin A defect (cblA) (OMIM #25,110), cobalamin B defect (cblB) (OMIM #251,110) and related conditions.

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Data are presented from a US natural history protocol that evaluated subjects with different types of MMA including mut-type (N = 91), cblB-type (15), and cblA-type MMA (17)

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants affecting MMUT and adenosylcobalamin-handling genes reduce catalytic conversion capacity for methylmalonyl-CoA utilization.

evidence.reference_title: Proposed guidelines for the diagnosis and management of methylmalonic and propionic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemia (MMA/PA) are inborn errors of metabolism characterized by accumulation of propionic acid and/or methylmalonic acid due to deficiency of methylmalonyl-CoA mutase (MUT) or propionyl-CoA carboxylase (PCC).

nodes.description: Recurrent high-anion-gap metabolic acidosis, often presenting in the neonatal period.

evidence.reference_title: Proposed guidelines for the diagnosis and management of methylmalonic and propionic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Patients present either shortly after birth with acute deterioration, metabolic acidosis and hyperammonemia

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Canonical metabolic markers, such as serum methylmalonic acid and propionylcarnitine, are variable and affected by dietary intake and renal function.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic acid in aging and disease.
evidence.snippet: MMA is normally present at low levels in the body, but increased levels can come from different sources, such as vitamin B12 deficiency, genetic mutations in enzymes related to the propionate pathway

readouts.interpretation: Increased serum or urinary methylmalonic acid reports the blocked methylmalonyl-CoA mutase/cofactor pathway.

nodes.description: Accumulating primary and secondary organic acid metabolites plus deficient succinyl-CoA input disrupt the TCA cycle, oxidative phosphorylation, and fatty-acid-derived energy generation, creating energetic vulnerability in high-demand organs.

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, NADH to ubiquinone

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: End-organ injury occurs due to both primary toxicity of both the accumulating primary and secondary metabolites and deficiency of succinyl-CoA resulting in Kreb cycle and oxidative phosphorylation dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: The impact of metabolic stressors on mitochondrial homeostasis in a renal epithelial cell model of methylmalonic aciduria.
evidence.snippet: Changes in tricarboxylic acid cycle metabolites and impaired energy generation from fatty acid oxidation were detected.

nodes.description: Movement disorders can occur as chronic complications and as sequelae of metabolic strokes in MMA/PA.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Chronic complications include poor growth, movement disorders, progressive spastic quadraparesis, epilepsy, cardiac dysfunction (PA>MMA), progressive renal disease (MMA), osteopenia/osteoporosis, vision loss (MMA>PA), and functional immunodeficiency

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Movement disorders or spastic quadra- or paraparesis, potential sequelae of metabolic strokes, should be managed in collaboration with a neurologist and/or physiatrist

nodes.description: Accumulated methylmalonic acid and related metabolites, secondary mitochondrial energy failure, and acute metabolic crises injure brain tissue, particularly basal ganglia circuits, producing developmental delay, cognitive impairment, epilepsy, and metabolic stroke-like injury.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Patients with organic acidopathies suffer metabolic brain injury that targets specific regions of the basal ganglia in a distinctive pattern

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic acidemia: Neurodevelopment and neuroimaging.
evidence.snippet: The accumulation of methylmalonic acid and other metabolites in the body of patients causes brain tissue damage, which can manifest as various degrees of intellectual disability and severe neurological dysfunction.

nodes.description: Optic neuropathy has been reported as a long-term complication, attributed to mitochondrial dysfunction in the optic nerve.

evidence.reference_title: Optic neuropathy in classical methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Eleven (52.4%) patients had optic neuropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: not entirely prevent brain injury or the development of optic neuropathy and cardiac disease

nodes.description: Osteoporosis is reported as a chronic skeletal complication in MMA/PA, requiring bone-density surveillance.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Although the specific mechanisms associated with bone health in OAs remain incompletely investigated, patients with PA and MMA are at significantly increased risk for osteopenia or osteoporosis that their age-matched peers, with and without renal disease

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: If pathological fractures or significant osteopenia/osteoporosis are detected on screening, more robust monitoring for bone density and response to interventions are indicated.

nodes.description: Acute pancreatitis is a recognized complication, occurring during or independent of metabolic decompensation episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Pancreatitis in patients with organic acidemias.
evidence.snippet: Five had methylmalonic acidemia, three had isovaleric acidemia, and one had maple syrup urine disease.

evidence.reference_title: Pancreatitis in patients with organic acidemias.
evidence.snippet: Acute or chronic pancreatitis may complicate branched-chain organic acidemias and must be considered in the assessment of patients with these disorders who have acute clinical deterioration and vomiting, abdominal pain, encephalopathy or shock, or milder symptoms.

nodes.description: Chronic growth impairment due to metabolic instability, dietary restrictions, and feeding intolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Chronic complications include poor growth, movement disorders, progressive spastic quadraparesis, epilepsy, cardiac dysfunction (PA>MMA), progressive renal disease (MMA), osteopenia/osteoporosis, vision loss (MMA>PA), and functional immunodeficiency

nodes.description: Acquired long QT syndrome is a recognized arrhythmia risk in MMA, associated with metabolic decompensation and chronic disease.

evidence.reference_title: Cardiac Involvement in Classical Organic Acidurias: Clinical Profile and Outcome in a Pediatric Cohort.
evidence.snippet: Cardiac anomalies were found in 23/60 OA patients, all with PA or MMA, represented by DCM (17/23 patients) and/or acquired long QT syndrome (3/23 patients).

evidence.reference_title: Biomarkers to predict disease progression and therapeutic response in isolated methylmalonic acidemia.
evidence.snippet: Canonical metabolic markers, such as serum methylmalonic acid and propionylcarnitine, are variable and affected by dietary intake and renal function.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical outcomes of patients with mut-type methylmalonic acidemia identified through expanded newborn screening in China.
evidence.snippet: NBS-detected patients showed younger age at diagnosis, less incidence of disease onset, better responsiveness of vitamin B12, younger age at start of treatment, lower levels of biochemical features before and after treatment

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Diagnosis is based on clinical presentation and laboratory analysis, metabolic acidosis, ketoacidosis, lactic acidosis, hyperammonemia, hypoglycemia, pancytopenia, and elevated C3 acylcarnitines and organic acids in the urine.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated propionylcarnitine reports propionate-pathway metabolite accumulation used for MMA diagnosis and newborn screening.

nodes.description: Osteopenia or reduced bone mineral density is a chronic skeletal complication in MMA/PA; the mechanism is not fully established.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Although the specific mechanisms associated with bone health in OAs remain incompletely investigated, patients with PA and MMA are at significantly increased risk for osteopenia or osteoporosis that their age-matched peers, with and without renal disease

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Various pathologies including vertebral fusion anomalies, vertebral compression and fractures, as well as generalized osteopenia, have been observed.

nodes.description: In renal epithelial cells from MMA patients, metabolic stressors cause impaired mitophagy (reduced PINK1), shifted mitochondrial dynamics toward fission (elevated DRP1, reduced OPA1), compromised antioxidant defenses, and lowered glutathione ratios, contributing to progressive chronic kidney disease.
nodes.id: Renal mitochondrial stress and impaired mitophagy

evidence.reference_title: The impact of metabolic stressors on mitochondrial homeostasis in a renal epithelial cell model of methylmalonic aciduria.
evidence.snippet: Mitophagy was disabled in MMA-uria cells, while autophagy was highly active particularly under HP and I/V conditions. Mitochondrial dynamics were shifted towards fission.

evidence.reference_title: The impact of metabolic stressors on mitochondrial homeostasis in a renal epithelial cell model of methylmalonic aciduria.
evidence.snippet: Creatinine levels were increased and antioxidant stress defense was severely comprised in MMA-uria cells.

nodes.description: Seizures may occur particularly in the context of metabolic decompensation and basal ganglia injury.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Chronic complications include poor growth, movement disorders, progressive spastic quadraparesis, epilepsy, cardiac dysfunction (PA>MMA), progressive renal disease (MMA), osteopenia/osteoporosis, vision loss (MMA>PA), and functional immunodeficiency

nodes.description: Spastic quadri- or paraparesis is reported as a chronic complication and potential metabolic-stroke sequela in MMA/PA.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Chronic complications include poor growth, movement disorders, progressive spastic quadraparesis, epilepsy, cardiac dysfunction (PA>MMA), progressive renal disease (MMA), osteopenia/osteoporosis, vision loss (MMA>PA), and functional immunodeficiency

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: Movement disorders or spastic quadra- or paraparesis, potential sequelae of metabolic strokes, should be managed in collaboration with a neurologist and/or physiatrist

nodes.description: Recurrent emesis during catabolic stress and metabolic decompensation episodes.

evidence.reference_title: Methylmalonic and propionic acidemias: clinical management update.
evidence.snippet: With intercurrent illness, PA and MMA patients typically present with nausea and vomiting, worsening anorexia, and encephalopathy

nodes.description: Partial residual methylmalonyl-CoA mutase activity. Some forms show partial response to hydroxocobalamin.
Complete loss of methylmalonyl-CoA mutase activity. Non-B12 responsive. Associated with the most severe phenotype and worst neuropsychological outcomes.
